title: Oreopanax argentatus
taxonomy: Oreopanax argentatus is classified within the family Araliaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the ginseng or ivy family. This family consists of approximately 70 genera and 1,600 species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Oreopanax argentatus is native to Central and South America, and its classification as a member of the Araliaceae family suggests a close relationship to other plants in this group. Overall, the classification of Oreopanax argentatus as a member of the Araliaceae family highlights its evolutionary relationship to other plants in this diverse and widespread family.

intro: Oreopanax argentatus, also known as the Silver Shield Fern, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with benefits including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of digestive issues, and the treatment of fever.
